Document:
[color=#ffff00][head=3]Section 9: Command, Security, TDF and Self-Antag Guidelines[/head][/color]
[color=#a4885c]9.1[/color] Command, Security, TDF and players self-antagging are expected maintain a high roleplay standard, and be very aware of escalation guidelines. You will be held to a higher standard in these roles. ★
[color=#a4885c]9.2[/color] Command, Security, and TDF members are required to know Sector Law and Standard Operating Procedures. ★
[color=#a4885c]9.3[/color] Do not abuse your position of power. This includes taking unearned payments or utilizing a Command/Security/Faction ship when not playing one of those roles. [color=#f1c232]★★[/color]
- [color=#a4885c]9.3A[/color] Command, Security, and TDF members are permitted to accept bribes in exchange for special treatment, but such actions will be scrutinised if they impact your faction significantly.
[color=#a4885c]9.4[/color] You are expected to roleplay with others and respond to situations that require your role to function. ★
- [color=#a4885c]9.4A[/color] Self-antags are especially required to roleplay and understand escalation rules. If you're intending to be in any way hostile to others you must follow the Escalation Guidelines.
[color=#a4885c]9.5[/color] If you are in a Command role, inform your department and Command before entering cryosleep or disconnecting. ★
[color=#a4885c]9.6[/color] Characters in Command roles must be the sort of character that could reasonably have attained their position and can reasonably perform their duties. If you are playing a character with disabilities in a position that other players rely on, it is your responsibility to ensure that you and your character can work around those barriers. ★
[color=#a4885c]9.7[/color] Do not utilise Expeditions as a means of escaping from the consequences of antagonistic activity. When self-antagging your are expected to be find-able within the sector. [color=#f1c232]★★[/color]
